seoTitle: How to Edit or Change a Product's Price on Gumroad
metaDescription: Learn how to update the price of an existing Gumroad product, including switching to pay-what-you-want pricing with a minimum floor.
intro: Prices aren't fixed forever on Gumroad, and adjusting them is a normal part of running a storefront as you test what works. This guide covers opening an existing listing, changing the base price, and optionally enabling flexible pricing like pay-what-you-want.

steps.heading: Open your product from the dashboard
steps.body: Go to your products list and select the item whose price you want to change.

steps.heading: Find the pricing field
steps.body: Locate the price input inside the product editor, where your current amount is displayed.

steps.heading: Enter the new price
steps.body: Clear the existing value and type in your updated price for the product.

steps.heading: Consider pay-what-you-want pricing
steps.body: If you'd rather offer flexible pricing, toggle that option and set a minimum floor price.

steps.heading: Save your changes
steps.body: Confirm the update so the new price goes live on your storefront immediately.

faq.question: Will changing the price affect past buyers?
faq.answer: No, a price change only applies to future purchases, not to sales that already happened.

faq.question: What is pay-what-you-want pricing on Gumroad?
faq.answer: It lets buyers choose their own price above a minimum floor you set, giving them flexibility while protecting your baseline revenue.

faq.question: How quickly does a price change take effect?
faq.answer: Once you save the update, the new price is reflected immediately on your live product page.

faq.question: Can I set different prices for different currencies?
faq.answer: Gumroad handles currency conversion based on your base price and the buyer's local currency at checkout.
